# WriteKit — MCP-native blogging platform

> WriteKit lets developers manage a blog entirely through an AI assistant using the Model Context Protocol. No editor, no dashboard.

## Quick start

```
claude mcp add --transport http writekit https://writekit.dev/mcp
```

Authentication happens automatically via OAuth (GitHub, Google, or Discord) on first connect.

## MCP Server URL

https://writekit.dev/mcp

## Tools

### create_post
Create a new blog post (starts as draft).
- Required: title (string), content (string, rich markdown)
- Optional: excerpt (string), tags (string[]), slug (string), tenant_id (string)
- Returns: post ID, slug, preview URL
- Content supports: GFM, tables, footnotes, syntax-highlighted code blocks, callout blocks, media embeds, D2 diagrams, images, raw HTML

### update_post
Update an existing post. Partial updates — only send fields to change.
- Required: id (string)
- Optional: title, content, excerpt, tags, slug, tenant_id
- Returns: updated post info, preview URL

### delete_post
Permanently delete a post and its comments.
- Required: id (string)
- Optional: tenant_id

### publish_post
Publish a draft post. Sets status=published, published_at=now.
- Required: id (string)
- Optional: tenant_id
- Returns: live URL at {subdomain}.writekit.dev/posts/{slug}

### unpublish_post
Revert a published post to draft.
- Required: id (string)
- Optional: tenant_id

### list_posts
List posts with optional filters.
- Optional: status (draft|published), tag (string), limit (int), tenant_id
- Returns: titles, slugs, statuses, tags, published dates

### get_post
Get a single post with full markdown content.
- Required: id (string) OR slug (string)
- Optional: tenant_id
- Returns: full post including raw markdown content

### search_posts
Full-text search across post titles and content (FTS5).
- Required: query (string)
- Optional: tenant_id

### list_comments
List comments on a specific post.
- Required: post_id (string)
- Optional: tenant_id

### delete_comment
Delete a comment.
- Required: id (string)
- Optional: tenant_id

### get_settings
Get all blog settings (title, description, code_theme).
- Optional: tenant_id

### update_settings
Update blog settings. Partial — only send keys to change.
- Optional: title (string), description (string), code_theme (string), tenant_id

## Prompts

### write_post
Generate a well-structured blog post about a topic.
- Required: topic (string) — What to write about
- Optional: audience (string) — Target audience (e.g., beginners, senior devs)
- Optional: style (string) — Writing style (e.g., tutorial, opinion, guide)

## Resources

- writekit://blog/stats — post counts, comment counts
- writekit://blog/settings — current blog settings as JSON
- writekit://blog/recent-posts — last 10 published posts
- writekit://blog/drafts — all draft posts
- writekit://blog/recent-comments — recent comments across all posts

## Tenant resolution

If a user has one blog, it's selected automatically. If multiple blogs exist, pass tenant_id on each tool call.

## Content format

Posts use Markdown rendered with Goldmark and Chroma syntax highlighting. Supported features:

### Basic Markdown
- Headings (# through ######) with auto-generated IDs
- **Bold**, *italic*, ~~strikethrough~~
- Links, images (by URL)
- Ordered/unordered lists, task lists
- Tables (GFM), horizontal rules, footnotes ([^1])
- Raw HTML

### Syntax-highlighted code blocks
Use fenced code blocks with language tags for rich rendering with language icons and copy buttons:
```go
func main() {
    fmt.Println("Hello, world!")
}
```
Supported languages include Go, Python, JavaScript, TypeScript, Rust, Java, Ruby, and 50+ more. Add a title with `title="filename.go"`.

### Callout blocks
Start a blockquote with a type marker for styled alert boxes:
```
> [!NOTE] This renders as a blue info box with an icon
> [!TIP] Green tip box — great for best practices
> [!WARNING] Yellow warning box — watch out!
> [!DANGER] Red danger box — critical issues
```
Also supports: [!INFO], [!IMPORTANT], [!CAUTION]

### Media embeds
Use `<embed src="url" />` for rich media:
- YouTube — renders thumbnail with play button, loads iframe on click
- Spotify — track, album, playlist, episode, or show embeds
- SoundCloud — player embed
- Twitter/X — tweet embed with widgets.js
- GitHub Gists — loaded dynamically

### D2 diagrams
Use `d2` code blocks for architecture and flow diagrams:
```d2
client -> server: HTTP request
server -> db: query
db -> server: results
server -> client: JSON response
```

## Blog URLs

- Blog: https://{subdomain}.writekit.dev
- Post: https://{subdomain}.writekit.dev/posts/{slug}
- Tag: https://{subdomain}.writekit.dev/tags/{tag}
- Search: https://{subdomain}.writekit.dev/search?q={query}
- RSS: https://{subdomain}.writekit.dev/feed.xml
- Preview: https://{subdomain}.writekit.dev/preview/{token} (expiring)

## Example prompts

- "Write a tutorial post about Go error handling with code examples and callout tips"
- "Create a post comparing React and Svelte with a comparison table"
- "Write a guide to setting up Docker with a D2 architecture diagram"
- "Show me all my draft posts"
- "Publish my post about Go concurrency"
- "Update my latest post — add a warning callout about common pitfalls"
- "Are there any new comments?"
- "Change my blog title to 'Ship Fast, Write Often'"
